summary: Designed 5 distinct cultures for story 001: Imperial Heartland Culture (CUL-001) with seven-tier hierarchy, the Keeping religion, Oathbrand social erasure, and inter-species taboos; Fae Culture of the Cerith Court and the Seep (CUL-002) with contractual social organization, the Reckoning spiritual practice, binding kill-contract mechanics, and the concept of love as structural failure; Eastern Border Culture of the Garrison Coast (CUL-003) with military atrophy, pre-Keeping folk religion, and pragmatic tolerance of the Oathbranded; The Wending Communities (CUL-004) with rotational habitation, consensus governance, fluid gender understanding, and Listening practice; Rivenmarch Freeholders (CUL-005) with radical impermanence, the Grounding daily practice, chosen-name-only identity, and no formal authority structures. Cultures create meaningful romantic obstacles: the empire's social erasure of the Oathbranded and stigma against fae-human intimacy, the Cerith Court's treatment of love as operational compromise, and the journey's cultural gradient from rigid hierarchy to radical autonomy mirroring the romance arc. 12 cross-cultural social rules document how food-sharing, naming conventions, and institutional constraints shape the romance at each geographic stage. All naming conventions defined per culture. No prohibited placeholder names used.

romanticObstacles: Oathbranded social erasure prevents Kaelith from being treated as an equal in imperial territory
Inter-species intimacy taboo makes any visible relationship between Kaelith and Lirien socially radioactive for Kaelith
Fae contractual culture treats love as structural failure — Lirien falling in love is a symptom of damage by her culture's standards
The kill contract physically prevents Lirien from returning home without completion — love cannot dissolve the contract
Fae radical omission (not lying but not disclosing) creates the conditions for the Black Moment when the kill contract is revealed
metadata.culturalGradient: East-to-west journey moves from imperial rigidity through forest consensus to radical impermanence, mirroring Pallor gradient and romance arc
